item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
The first image that was used in this article is non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from the Monolithic Dome Institute, which only allows its content to be used for non-commercial use per their Terms of Service. Thus, it has been removed. The other two images were replaced with cc-compliant replacements, and are as follows:

Source: Wikimedia Commons
License: Public Domain
Title: Tabor CZ aerial old town from north B1.jpg
Author: Rudolf Kukačka (Ruda)
Release year: 2007

Source: Flickr
License: CC BY-SA 2.0
Title: fieldscape
Author: Victor Szalvay (vsz)
Release year: 2006

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While I feel like it has some noticeable weak points, I liked it enough to upvote anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: carefully

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… if they can't be destroyed, and they already ate a town…

How'd the Foundation get them into a locker?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I… I just don't get it. They're molecules that dismantle buildings because they just don't like them? And they sing?! Like they're the goddamn dwarves from Snow White?! I'm sorry but that's just too goofy and not in a good way.

Also, aren't all organisms "molecular organisms"? How is this notable? Or are they organisms made of a single molecule, also known as "not an organism"?

And what's the deal with the two blatantly unrelated pictures in the first addendum? Am I supposed to conclude they're "before and after" pictures? Because it's very obvious they aren't and there isn't much point to such a comparison if you're using completely different camera angles anyway.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The song absolutely and completely destroyed it for me. It turns something that was primeval and scary into something which was created by people with a sense of humour. Basically like AWCY?. Shame, because I really liked it otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
How did the Foundation cover up an entire town vanishing like that?

Still, upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno. I never saw this as horrifying, but kinda whimsical. In a good way.

Not sure why I had it downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or, y'know, they were removed by Foundation agents after the town was dismantled since they were no longer active, and then the area was turned into farmland. But I like the idea of a bunch of nanobots walking around with lawnmowers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If they're so keen on Nature, why did they plow and plant the fields in careful rows and mow all the lawns in neat rectangles after dismantling the human structures? What, Agriculture doesn't count? By that logic, if we all lived in trees, would it leave us alone? They've obviously been tending that land ever SINCE reclaiming it, or else other humans have moved in and made it farmland, because otherwise it should be overgrown with random weeds.

It just raises too many questions.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with the people who said this is more fundamentally horrifying without the song. But I like the version with the song too, and find it a bit creepier, to balance out it being less horrifying. Either way, nicely done. +1'ed

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"It's a Small World" is the creepiest ride I have ever been on.

Not understanding how people don't see "It's a Small World" as creepy. The song or the ride.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JwfCRAtkYEI

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "It's a Small World" is the creepiest ride I have ever been on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, but this is some 'It's a Small World' shit

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll do a bit of a mix. Added a footnote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: wow, that was faster than expected. this is great, good work :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When introduced to an artificial structure, SCP-1887 will proceed to disassemble the construct at a quantum level through an unknown process. The release of energy from the splitting of the nucleus is then absorbed by SCP-1887.

Splitting a nucleus only releases energy if it's of an element heavier than iron. Splitting an atom of iron or anything lighter takes energy. The SCP could make energy by one of the following methods:

  1. Fusing atoms lighter than iron.
  2. Turning matter directly into energy. This would require destroying some atoms, which might be against the ethos of the SCP, but only a tiny number of atoms would need to be destroyed.
  3. The anomalous disassembly method could produce energy is a manner inconsistatnt with physics as currently understood.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was reading through comments going, "What song?" Then I went back, didn't even have to refresh, and there was a song! And it was dumb. And then I came back here and now there is no more song.

That's life for ya.

Anyway, I upvoted the 'original' idea because, while not the most pants-shitting SCP, it's certainly a very good little setup. I do wonder at the Keter designation; the clay pot and soil seem to contain these things, right?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry Aburr. At even more popular request I brought back the song. But I put in an extra verse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted in the interim, but it's still there, as a heads-up. Nice article, good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok, at popular request I deleted the song. Revise your votes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno if I expressed anything better than you did, the way you describe this thing destroying all of humanity's creations to reduce us to animals makes me think of this in an entirely new way. That's a damned chilling thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, Sorts expressed this much better than I did. Purely on the level of "thing that breaks down things people make", they're similar, but this has a very different feel and comes from a very different direction than 804. This is protean, natural, more of an intrinsic process given form than an Evil Monster. It's decay of a specifically disheartening kind; the fact that it's not mentioned to have any effect on human bodies makes it worse, actually, since it's fundamentally reducing us to animals, not even special enough to target. It's unnerving, in a highly personal Ozymandian way. The song spoils it, for me, not so much because it shows hostility as because it creates agency. It's an existential threat, not a practical one, and as such its emphasis should be on process, not preference. It undermines the point to make it deliberate, since it still says all the accoutrements of humanity are worth destroying, instead of being pointlessly effaced by time regardless.

Or that's my opinion, anyways. 804 is only the same thing on an exceedingly simple level.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
804 is about people who hate humanity so much they want to see it scrubbed off the face of the earth being granted their wish. This is entirely different from that because there's no human agency involved.

That's why the song ruins this one for me though, because it gives a what is otherwise a faceless non-human force a human voice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What Zeke said, except without the Damascene moment. This is similar to 804, as somebody noted, although I think it's sufficiently different, and sufficiently better, to receive an upvote just on those merits…but the song, oh man, the song. The song kills it for me. No vote, but I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was pleased with it up until the song. On first read, I went "The song killed it for me. Then, after I closed the page, I thought back to the idea of that song being sung as stuff slowly melted into piles of red goop. I shuddered, re-opened, and upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The idea of a race of itty bitty super-environmentalist that can deconstruct anything made by human hands makes my hair prickle, just a little.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The song scares me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm a sucker for the hippy scips, so perhaps it's no surprise that I like this one- but I do like it! I like how the affect isn't even really illustrated in the article, but is communicated by the pictures.
Also, this will now be in my mind as an alternate origin theory for my own SCP-1009.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You lost me at the singing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think disassembly at the quantum level makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's well-executed but I'm not sure how I feel about the idea. It seems like 804 + grey goo. No vote atm.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Keter Disney Villains? Now I've seen everything…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What language is the song in?

I see this as faeries. Or something related to them, anyhow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Veryyy nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sic these little guys on some Church of the Broken God stuff, and watch the fireworks! Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That song is fantastic!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: loved everything about it but the song going over my head kept my from upvoting. After realizing it was the toaster i upvoted with the fury of a thousand suns.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just sayin, not all SCPs need to be horrifyingly scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Atomic ants who want to preserve nature and sing, how could I not upvote this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I loved that song as a kid. I also love this. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't quite vote on this. Yes, it's well written, and yes, the idea of some sort of eco-loving murder bacteria is interesting, but I just don't find it scary. This is largely due to me reading the little song in the voice of the dwarves from Snow White. I'd suggest you make it somewhat less…whimsical. Maybe redact a few parts there, while removing the redaction for the disappearing town.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whaaaat? Sal posting a mainlist SCP? What's going on?

Yes indeed. Props to Rhett for the name. Inspiration for this SCP: http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZLcRSIEtXwI
